title: drosera menziesii subsp. thysanosepala
intro: Drosera menziesii subsp. thysanosepala is a subspecies of the sundew plant, specifically a type of carnivorous plant native to the western coast of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant to help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing swelling and fighting off infections. Drosera menziesii subsp. thysanosepala is often prepared as a tincture or tea, and is also used in herbal remedies to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
